title: Comparing Fractions with the Same Denominator

steps.instruction: Compare 3/8 and 5/8. Are the denominators the same?
steps.work: Both have denominator 8. Yes, they're the same!
steps.explanation: When denominators match, the pieces are the same size.

steps.instruction: Since the pieces are the same size, more pieces means a larger fraction.
Use the greater-than or less-than symbol.
steps.explanation: 5/8 is the greater fraction because it has more equal-sized pieces.

title: Comparing Fractions with the Same Numerator

steps.instruction: Compare 1/3 and 1/5. Are the numerators the same?
steps.explanation: When numerators match, we're taking the same number of pieces.

steps.instruction: Which has bigger pieces: thirds or fifths?
steps.work: Dividing into 3 parts makes bigger pieces than dividing into 5 parts.
steps.explanation: Fewer equal parts means each part is bigger. A third of a pizza is bigger than a fifth.

steps.instruction: The fraction with bigger pieces is greater.
steps.explanation: Same number of pieces, but thirds are bigger than fifths, so 1/3 > 1/5.
